one_time_description: Robinson Huron Treaty Litigation Fund settlement receivable - past compensation from Federal and Ontario Provincial Governments arising from obligations under the Robinson Huron Treaty of 1850
economic_notes: First Nation holds 33.333% interest in Shwe Miikaan Limited Partnership (modified equity). Multiple Feed-in-Tariff solar partnerships held at nil value. Gas bar and convenience store operations. Fish harvesting, bush clearing, youth employment programs. Henvey Inlet Transmission revenues. Casino Rama revenue received.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on multiple grounds: (1) inventory count incomplete for gas bar and convenience store - unable to verify inventory quantities or cost of sales; (2) PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations not adopted - management unable to complete assessment of tangible capital asset retirement obligations; (3) Cannabis sales and expenses April 1 to November 1, 2023 unverifiable due to no tracking; (4) Gas sales December 1, 2023 to March 31, 2024 unverifiable. The $274,145,086 Robinson Huron Treaty settlement receivable is a massive one-time item that dominates financials - accumulated surplus jumps from $10.97M to $289.1M. OSR calculation includes Robinson Huron Treaty amount which inflates it significantly. Net financial assets positive only due to settlement receivable. Shwe Miikaan Limited Partnership is the sole GBE. Schedule of Remuneration not included in provided text. Capital expenses segment $191,281 included in expenses_infrastructure was left as community operations; capital segment kept separate at $191,281 but mapped infrastructure to community operations $1,601,963.
